title: Using fillable PDF's within monday file columns
excerpt:
It would be incredibly beneficial if we could use fillable PDF’s on the file columns within Monday. It’s time consuming to have to download the forms, fill them out, and re-upload them into monday. 
I’m no programmer, bu…

posts.author: eric.ward (Eric)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be incredibly beneficial if we could use fillable PDF’s on the file columns within Monday. It’s time consuming to have to download the forms, fill them out, and re-upload them into monday.

I’m no programmer, but I feel like this shouldn’t be too hard to fix.

posts.author: Renae-JTsCloud (Renae-upstream)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @eric.ward I know it’s not exactly what you’re seeking- but depending on the specificity required of your output- you could potentially solve this with a monday.com doc- or if it absolutely has to be a formatted pdf- there’s a couple workarounds we could do. I’ll attach a loom below as an example: Loom | Free Screen & Video Recording Software | Loom

If you want to chat further, feel free to shoot me an email:
renae@upstreamtech.io
